caption.text: Your user has just pulled out their bank card,
they’re super motivated and you just tell them thanks.
The next-to-last page, the payment page, is one of the most neglected screens in all SaaS.
even though it’s the best psychological moment
of the whole user journey. Because they’re in a positive emotional state.
So this is the moment to turn that page from
a confirmation into an engagement tool.
First thing, do a celebration. Not just “payment confirmed,” but “welcome,
you’ve just made the best choice.”
The user has to feel good about their decision.
Second thing, create anticipation and show them
clearly what they’ll be able to do now.
Not the features, but the results.
You’ll be able to do X, Y,
Z, etc. And third thing,
give them an immediate action. Just one clear, direct CTA. For example,
“start now” or “create my first project.”
The goal is to guide them to their first win while they’re motivated.
The next-to-last page, the payment page, isn’t just a technical confirmation,
it’s the start of the experience.
That’s where you turn a buyer into an active user
and it changes your retention.
